pi_items.source: Verified via setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which target the 'sakila' database directly.
Verified via task.json prompt and export_result.sh which explicitly checks for the view 'v_audit_underpayments'.
Verified via export_result.sh which reads from CSV_PATH='/home/ga/Documents/exports/underpayment_audit.csv'.
Verified via setup_task.sh which explicitly executes 'UPDATE payment ... SET p.amount = 0.01;'.

Verified via setup_task.sh which limits the corruption updates to exactly 40 records (which is >= 30). The Sakila DB is sufficiently large to guarantee 40 records match the condition rental_rate > 2.99.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the synthetic Sakila sample database. A data corruption script (setup_task.sh) introduces an anomaly by updating exactly 40 payment records (for films with a rental rate > $2.99) to an amount of $0.01. The task requires the agent to identify these anomalies via a SQL view named 'v_audit_underpayments', export them to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/underpayment_audit.csv', and revert the corrupted 'amount' values back to their original 'rental_rate' while leaving valid records untouched.
